mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and signals aggregate demand strength, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS momentum on 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any trailing 2-year window, or the sign of the predictive relationship flips during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth reflects a strengthening labor market, boosting household income and consumer confidence, which translates to positive equity market returns over the monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after positive PAYEMS surprises does not exceed the unconditional mean return by at least 50 basis points on average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a 4% threshold, the tighter monetary policy increases discount rates on equity cash flows and raises the opportunity cost of holding stocks, pressuring S&P 500 valuations downward over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, average 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint increase of both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary-like pressure that amplifies discount rate sensitivity and erodes real earnings expectations, producing stronger negative equity returns than either series alone would predict.
regime_hint: high inflation and rising rate environment
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) has a rank IC below 0.03 on 20-day forward returns over any 3-year window, or the signal does not materially outperform the individual series in a pairwise backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (UNRATE below 4%), further declines in the unemployment rate signal building wage pressures that force the Federal Reserve to preemptively tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as growth decelerates.
failure_condition: During regimes where UNRATE is below 4%, the rank correlation between month-over-month declines in UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals synchronized expansion in both residential construction and manufacturing output, indicating broad-based economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined rank IC of HOUST and INDPRO momentum (both positive) on 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year window, or the interaction yields no additional predictive power over univariate signals.

mechanism_narrative: Trend growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects the underlying expansion of the economy; sustained increases in output raise corporate profits and investor risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the quarter following the data release.
failure_condition: The sequential trend in GDPC1 (quarter-over-quarter) explains less than 5% of the variance in subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 10-year period, or the sign of the relationship becomes negative in post-recession recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceed the 160,000-threshold level, it confirms above-trend job creation that sustains household income and consumer spending, lifting equity valuations over the near-term horizon.
failure_condition: Following PAYEMS readings above 160,000, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year period, or the hit rate of positive returns remains below 60%.
